example: An anonymous benefactor donated a million dollars to the university.
mnemonic: bene(좋은) + fact(하다) + or = 좋은 일을 하는 사람 → 후원자. bene(좋은): benefit, benevolent. fac(하다): factory, manufacture

example: The team's performance this season has been abysmal.
mnemonic: abyss(심연, 그리스어 abyssos = 바닥 없는) + mal = 심연처럼 깊은 → 최악의. a(없는) + byssos(바닥)

example: The philosopher argued that morality is immanent in human nature.
mnemonic: im(안에) + man(머물다, 라틴어 manere) + ent = 안에 머무는 → 내재하는. man(머물다): remain, permanent, mansion

example: His injury precluded him from participating in the championship match.
mnemonic: pre(미리) + clude(닫다, 라틴어 claudere) = 미리 닫다 → 배제하다. clud(닫다): include, exclude, conclude, seclude

example: The politician's risible excuse for his absence fooled no one.
mnemonic: ris(웃다, 라틴어 ridere) + ible = 웃을 만한 → 우스꽝스러운. ris/rid(웃다): ridicule, deride, risibility

example: The media was accused of presenting a partisan view of the election.
mnemonic: part(부분, 파벌) + isan = 한 파벌에 속한 → 당파적인. part(부분): partial, participate

example: The warlord hired mercenary soldiers to fight on his behalf.
mnemonic: mercen(보수, 라틴어 merces=임금) + ary = 보수를 위한 → 용병. merc(상업): merchant, mercury, commerce

example: Each artist has unique idiosyncrasies that define their creative process.
mnemonic: idio(자기만의, 그리스어 idios) + syn(함께) + crasy(섞임) = 자기만의 독특한 혼합 → 기벽

example: The monk abnegated all worldly pleasures to pursue spiritual growth.
mnemonic: ab(떨어져) + neg(부정하다, 라틴어 negare) + ate = 부정하여 떨어뜨리다 → 포기하다. neg(부정): negate, negative, deny

example: The evanescent beauty of cherry blossoms attracts millions of visitors.
mnemonic: e(밖으로) + van(사라지다, 라틴어 vanescere) + escent = 사라져가는 → 덧없는. van(사라지다): vanish, vain

example: The plaintive cry of the bird echoed through the empty forest.
mnemonic: plaint(불평, 탄식, 라틴어 plangere=가슴을 치다) + ive = 탄식하는 → 슬픈. plaint(탄식): complaint, plaintiff

example: The refugees endured terrible privation during their long journey.
mnemonic: priv(빼앗다, 라틴어 privare) + ation = 빼앗긴 상태 → 결핍. priv(빼앗다): deprive, private(개인적인=타인에게서 빼앗긴)

example: The monotonous hum of the machinery made the workers drowsy.
mnemonic: mono(하나) + ton(음, 그리스어 tonos) + ous = 하나의 음만 있는 → 단조로운. mono(하나): monologue, monopoly

example: The refulgent sunrise over the ocean filled the travelers with awe.
mnemonic: re(다시, 뒤로) + fulg(빛나다, 라틴어 fulgere) + ent = 빛을 되쏘는 → 눈부시게 빛나는. fulg(빛나다): fulgent, effulgent

example: The civil war sundered the nation into two hostile factions.
mnemonic: 고대 영어 sundrian(분리하다). 갈라놓다 → 분리하다. asunder(산산이)와 같은 어원

example: The querulous patient complained about everything from the food to the nurses.
mnemonic: quer(불평하다, 라틴어 queri) + ulous = 불평이 많은 → 투덜거리는. quer(불평): quarrel, query

example: A single mistake can mar an otherwise perfect record.
mnemonic: 고대 영어 merran(방해하다, 손상시키다). 짧고 강한 단어: 손상시키다 → 망치다

example: The government debased the currency by printing excessive amounts of money.
mnemonic: de(아래로) + base(기초, 낮은) = 아래로 낮추다 → 가치를 떨어뜨리다. base(낮은): abase, basement

example: The byzantine tax code confused even the most experienced accountants.
mnemonic: Byzantium(비잔티움 제국)에서 유래. 비잔티움 제국의 정치가 매우 복잡했던 것에서 → 복잡한, 난해한

example: The laws of physics are considered immutable by most scientists.
mnemonic: '이뮤터블' → 'im(아닌)+mutable(변할 수 있는)' 변할 수 없는 불변!

example: The genteel manners of the old aristocracy have largely disappeared.
mnemonic: gent(태어나다, 종족) + eel = 좋은 가문에서 태어난 → 품위 있는. gent(태어남): gentle, gentry, generate

example: The monks performed their morning ablutions before entering the temple.
mnemonic: ab(떨어져) + lut(씻다, 라틴어 luere) + ion = 씻어내는 행위 → 세정. lu/lav(씻다): dilute, lavatory, deluge

example: She took umbrage at the suggestion that she was not qualified.
mnemonic: umbr(그림자, 라틴어 umbra) + age = 그림자가 드리우듯 → 불쾌감. umbr(그림자): umbrella, penumbra, adumbrate

example: The monk eschewed all worldly pleasures in pursuit of spiritual enlightenment.
mnemonic: 고대 프랑스어 eschiver(피하다), 게르만어 skiuhan(겁내다)에서 유래 → 의식적으로 피하다. shy(수줍은)와 같은 어원

example: The teacher tried to disabuse her students of common scientific myths.
mnemonic: dis(제거) + abuse(남용, 잘못된 사용) = 잘못된 생각을 제거하다 → 바로잡다

example: His fatuous remarks during the meeting embarrassed everyone present.
mnemonic: fatu(어리석은, 라틴어 fatuus) + ous = 어리석은. infatuate(열중시키다=어리석게 만들다)와 같은 어근

example: The company tried to mollify angry customers with gift vouchers.
mnemonic: moll(부드러운, 라틴어 mollis) + ify = 부드럽게 만들다 → 달래다. moll(부드러운): emollient

example: The author's florid prose style is not to everyone's taste.
mnemonic: '플로리드' → 'flor(꽃)+id' 꽃처럼 화려한!

example: The idea of censorship is anathema to most journalists.
mnemonic: 그리스어 anathema(봉헌물 → 저주받은 것). ana(위로) + thema(놓다) = 신에게 바쳐 저주한 것 → 혐오의 대상

example: The editor truncated the article to fit the available space.
mnemonic: trunc(줄기, 라틴어 truncus) + ate = 줄기를 자르다 → 절단하다. trunk(줄기, 몸통)와 같은 어원

example: The officious clerk insisted on checking every form three times.
mnemonic: offic(직무, 라틴어 officium) + ious = 직무에 과도하게 열심인 → 참견하는. office(사무실)와 같은 어근

example: The economic problems were endogenous, caused by the country's own policies.
mnemonic: endo(안에, 그리스어) + gen(생기다) + ous = 안에서 생기는 → 내인성의. gen(생기다): gene, generate. endo(안에): endoscopy

example: The medieval chronicler recorded events from the king's reign in detail.
mnemonic: chron(시간, 그리스어 chronos) + icler = 시간 순서대로 기록하는 사람 → 연대기 작가. chron(시간): chronology, synchronize

example: The ancient civilization is considered the progenitor of modern democratic thought.
mnemonic: pro(앞으로) + genit(낳다, 라틴어 gignere) + or = 앞서 낳은 자 → 조상. gen(낳다): generate, genesis, progeny, congenital

example: Fame in the entertainment industry is often ephemeral.
mnemonic: '이페머럴' → 'epi(위에)+hemer(하루)' 하루만 위에 있는 덧없는!

example: Public interest in the scandal began to wane after several weeks.
mnemonic: 고대 영어 wanian(줄어들다)에서 유래. 달이 이지러지다 → 약해지다. wax and wane(차고 기울다)

example: The volcano remained quiescent for centuries before suddenly erupting.
mnemonic: quie(조용한, 라틴어 quies) + scent = 조용해지고 있는 → 잠잠한. quie(조용한): quiet, acquiesce(묵인하다)

example: The politician's hypocrisy was exposed when his private actions contradicted his public statements.
mnemonic: hypo(아래에) + crisy(판단하다, 그리스어 krinein) = 아래에서 연기하다 → 위선. 그리스어 hypokrites(배우, 위선자)

example: Many expatriates living abroad still maintain strong ties to their homeland.
mnemonic: '엑스패트리엇' → 'ex(밖)+patri(조국)+ate' 조국 밖으로 나간 국외 거주자!

example: The miscreants responsible for the vandalism were caught on camera.
mnemonic: mis(잘못된, 고대 프랑스어 mes) + creant(믿는, 라틴어 credere) = 잘못 믿는 → 악당. cred(믿다): creed, credit

example: Quick action by the authorities forestalled a potential disaster.
mnemonic: '포어스톨' → 'fore(미리)+stall(멈추다)' 미리 멈추어 방지!

example: He loved to pontificate about politics at every family gathering.
mnemonic: pontific(교황의, 라틴어 pontifex=다리를 놓는 자) + ate = 교황처럼 말하다 → 독단적으로 말하다

example: The judge issued a peremptory order to clear the courtroom.
mnemonic: per(완전히) + empt(취하다, 라틴어 emere=사다) + ory = 완전히 빼앗는 → 단호한. empt(취하다): exempt, preempt

example: The young actress handled the difficult interview with remarkable aplomb.
mnemonic: a(~에) + plomb(납, 프랑스어 plomb) = 추처럼 흔들리지 않는 → 침착함. plumb(납추): plumber, plummet

example: The fractious committee could not agree on a single proposal.
mnemonic: '프랙셔스' → 'fract(부수다)+ious' 부수듯 성마르고 다루기 힘든!

example: The archaeologist discovered that the artifact was spurious.
mnemonic: 라틴어 spurius(사생아, 가짜)에서 유래 → 진짜가 아닌 거짓의

example: The morning fog dissipated as the sun rose higher in the sky.
mnemonic: dis(사방으로) + sip(던지다, 라틴어 supare) + ate = 사방으로 던져 흩다 → 흩어지다, 낭비하다

example: The restive crowd grew increasingly impatient with the long delay.
mnemonic: rest(멈추다) + ive = 멈추기를 거부하는 → 안절부절못하는. 원래 말이 멈춰서 안 가려는 것에서 유래

example: Everyone has a few peccadilloes that they would rather keep private.
mnemonic: 스페인어 pecadillo(작은 죄), pecado(죄, 라틴어 peccatum). 작은 죄 → 사소한 잘못. pecc(죄): impeccable(흠잡을 데 없는)

example: The problem is multifaceted and requires a comprehensive solution.
mnemonic: multi(많은) + facet(면) + ed = 많은 면을 가진 → 다면적인
